images.scene_description_en: Interior of ruined school building. Overturned desks and chairs. Faded chalk on blackboard. Light penetrating the classroom. Quiet sense of loss.

images.scene_description_en: Ruined room interior. Light beams cutting through dust and debris. Light itself provides the motion. Composition that feels alive even as a still image.
images.scene_description_ja: 廃墟の部屋。光の筋（光線）が塵埃とともに差し込む。光そのものが「動く」演出として機能。静止画でも動きを感じる構図。

IMG014 must read as room-scale and intimate, not grand-scale ruin.

images.reason_approved_for_batch_2b: No flag words. Subject is a physical heritage plaque — factually appropriate for S021. The scene purpose (UNESCO tension/juxtaposition) is served by the visual of official recognition against physical decay. Critical: all text on plaque must be illegible/worn — prompt and negative prompt both enforce this. Corrected from PROMPT_017 content (tourist vessel, which belongs to S023/IMG019).
prompt_changes_from_queue: Added 'heavily tarnished and weathered with worn inscription that is illegible due to age and corrosion' to plaque description — removes ambiguity about text legibility
Negative prompt strengthened: 'legible text in any language, readable inscription' added explicitly
images.critical_note: The plaque text MUST be illegible. Generating a plaque with readable text risks: (1) inventing specific UNESCO declaration text not in the script, (2) falsely attributing specific language to the UNESCO designation. Negative prompt enforces this.
